summary: Matched or improved multiple stage-related functions across Corneria, Great Bay, Home Run Contest, Mushroom Kingdom, Jungle Japes, and Dream Land 64 stage files. The diff replaces placeholder decomp markers with C implementations, adds a Great Bay joint-to-collision table, recovers a Home Run ground-vars union arm and header signatures, and adjusts small stage-local config structs from int to s16 pairs.

key_files.role: Implemented grCorneria_801E0D30, grCorneria_801E0F6C, and grCorneria_801E2D90 for Great Fox movement, Arwing sequencing, and standable surface checks.
Implemented grGreatBay_801F660C and added grGb_803E4048 joint/collision index pairs used to update collision vertex positions from animated JObjs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGb_803E4048 table and grGreatBay_801F660C loop

key_files.role: Updated grGreatBay_801F660C prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void grGreatBay_801F660C(Ground_GObj*).
Implemented four near-identical Home Run Contest text-positioning functions and added grHr_804D6AE4 storage.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Home Run Contest function summary

key_files.role: Added lb forward include and recovered grHomeRun_8021EC58 signature as void* grHomeRun_8021EC58(u16).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grhomerun.h include and prototype update

key_files.role: Implemented fn_801FBF6C callback that finds a matching ? Block by item variable JObj pointer, asserts a ground object exists, and respawns/effects the block.
Implemented grOldKongo_8021005C, added baselib includes, split config struct into s16 x0/x2, set JObj flags, and randomized a ground var from configured range.
Implemented grOldPupupu_80210C7C using s16 config bounds and HSD_Randi to choose a wait/random value.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Dream Land 64 Whispy Woods summary

key_files.role: Added struct grHomeRun_GroundVars with xC4/xC6/xC8/xCC fields and added it to the Ground union.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grHomeRun_GroundVars addition and Ground union arm

what_changed: Removed placeholder decomp comments for at least 10 functions visible in the diff and replaced them with C implementations in stage source files.
Updated two headers to replace unknown prototypes with typed signatures for grGreatBay_801F660C and grHomeRun_8021EC58.
Introduced a Home Run-specific GroundVars struct and union arm while the implemented Home Run functions still also access gp->gv.unk for xC8/xCC.
Added Great Bay data table grGb_803E4048 as pairs of collision vertex IDs and JObj IDs, iterated with a natural for loop.
Added or adjusted baselib includes for JObj/random access in old-stage files.
Bot report recorded +1440 bytes matched code, 8 new 100% matches, and 6 improved unmatched/data items.

smart_moves.text: The Great Bay implementation uses a compact table plus for-loop for seven repeated collision mesh updates instead of duplicating seven call sequences.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGb_803E4048 and for (i = 0; i < 7; i++) in grGreatBay_801F660C

smart_moves.text: Recovered header prototypes alongside implementations rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for touched functions.
Stage-local config structs in groldkongo.c and groldpupupu.c were narrowed from one int field to s16 x0/x2 pairs, matching the random-bound access pattern in the new functions.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a stage function applies the same operation to several JObj/collision vertex pairs, a small stage-local pair table plus a counted loop can express the likely source shape and avoid repeated generated blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGreatBay_801F660C uses grGb_803E4048[][2] and for-loop

decomp_lessons.lesson: Recovering a function body can expose both the callee prototype and the needed forward include; update the owning header when the diff proves the signature.
Stage config data layout may need narrowing to halfword fields when recovered code reads paired s16 bounds for random range selection.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: groldkongo.c static struct changed from int x0 to s16 x0/s16 x2
raw_diff: groldpupupu.c static struct changed from int x0 to s16 x0/s16 x2

naming_conventions.name: grHomeRun_GroundVars
naming_conventions.convention: Stage-specific GroundVars union arms are named after the stage/module and added under struct Ground::gv when function bodies reveal recurring per-stage fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Ground union adds struct grHomeRun_GroundVars homerun

naming_conventions.name: grGb_803E4048
naming_conventions.convention: Address-style data names were retained for a newly exposed Great Bay data table rather than assigning unsupported semantic names.

naming_conventions.name: fn_801FBF6C
naming_conventions.convention: Address-style function names were retained where no semantic name was provided by the PR evidence.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: fn_801FBF6C listed by address-style name

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK(16) at the end of fn_801FBF6C, indicating a stack-shape matching tactic.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The bot report shows fn_801FBF6C improved to 93.72%, not a full match, so this tactic should not be generalized without narrower objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Preserved repeated Home Run text-positioning function bodies across four callbacks instead of factoring a helper.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists all four Home Run functions as 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The diff supports repetition as a matching shape in this PR, but it does not prove a global preference against helper extraction.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used explicit ternary random range expressions and an inline rand_inline helper to handle reversed/equal bounds while matching old-stage random initialization.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: groldpupupu.c ternary HSD_Randi range expression
raw_diff: groldkongo.c static inline int rand_inline
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Both implementations share the range-selection idea but not identical source shape.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used HSD_ASSERT for the Great Bay JObj null check, while fn_801FBF6C retains a raw __assert with file, line, and message.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR evidence does not explain why the raw __assert remained, and fn_801FBF6C was only 93.72% in the bot report.

review_feedback.feedback: Automated report recorded 8 new 100% matches: grCorneria_801E0F6C, grCorneria_801E2D90, grHomeRun_8021DF50, grHomeRun_8021E0D4, grHomeRun_8021E258, grHomeRun_8021E3DC, grCorneria_801E0D30, and grOldPupupu_80210C7C.
Automated report recorded improvements but not full matches for fn_801FBF6C at 93.72%, grOldKongo_8021005C at 99.76%, and grGreatBay_801F660C at 99.08%, plus data-section improvements.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issue_comment decomp-dev[bot] improvements in unmatched items

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments are present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching fn_801FBF6C PAD_STACK(16) or converting its raw __assert to HSD_ASSERT.
Search later changes to grGreatBay_801F660C or grGb_803E4048 to see whether the 99.08% improvement became a full match.
Search later changes to grOldKongo_8021005C and rand_inline to see whether the 99.76% improvement was finalized.
Search for later cleanup of repeated Home Run text-positioning functions or grHomeRun_GroundVars xC4/xC6/xC8/xCC field names.
Search for fn_8021E994 in PR 2318 artifacts or later PRs because it is listed in the PR body but not visible in the provided diff excerpt.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, merge state, counts, changed files
human_pr_text: summary and claimed fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions
notes: Changed-file metadata appears duplicated in metadata_json and changed_files.jsonl; unique paths were deduplicated in this record.

The PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, but the bot report says only 8 new matches and lists fn_801FBF6C, grOldKongo_8021005C, and grGreatBay_801F660C as less than 100%. The bot report was treated as stronger evidence for per-symbol match status.

The PR body lists fn_8021E994 in grhomerun.c, but the provided diff excerpt does not show an implementation for fn_8021E994; no file-specific claim was made for that symbol beyond noting the discrepancy.
No human reviewer comments were loaded, so reviewer intent and requested changes are unavailable.
The slice includes fuzzy/check report evidence but not narrow objdiff logs, build commands, or final source after merge beyond the diff.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use table-driven loops for repeated stage joint/collision updates when the repeated operation only varies by paired IDs.
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2318's grGreatBay_801F660C replaced a placeholder with a stage-local s16 pair table and a for-loop over seven entries, calling Ground_801C3FA4, asserting the JObj, reading its position, and updating mpVtxSetPos. This supports the existing natural-loops standard for repeated stage operations.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report grGreatBay_801F660C improved to 99.08%

accepted_candidate_records.title: Function-body recovery should drive header prototype cleanup.
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2318 updated grgreatbay.h and grhomerun.h prototypes after recovering implementations/call signatures, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with typed declarations.

source_update_candidates.title: Stage module path scope for PR 2318
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2318 touched stage/ground source under src/melee/gr, including grcorneria.c, grgreatbay.c/.h, grhomerun.c/.h, grinishie1.c, groldkongo.c, groldpupupu.c, and gr/types.h.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scope fact for routing future stage decomp intake and searches.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not treat fuzzy_match_percent claims in PR body as final per-symbol match evidence when bot reports disagree.
source_update_candidates.text: In PR 2318 the PR body claimed fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, but the decomp-dev bot report listed only 8 new 100% matches and several sub-100% improvements. Curator may consider documenting that automated per-symbol reports should be preserved when they conflict with broad PR-body verification claims.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Verification fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report with 8 new matches and 6 improvements
source_update_candidates.reason: This is an evidence-handling rule for curation/intake, not a source-code style rule; curator review is needed before adding it anywhere.

source_update_candidates.title: Require caution around PAD_STACK in partially matched functions.
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2318 used PAD_STACK(16) in fn_801FBF6C, while the bot report showed the function at 93.72% rather than a full match. This reinforces the existing matching-tactics standard that stack padding needs narrow verifier evidence and should not be generalized from a partial improvement.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential reinforcement/example for an existing standard, not a new accepted rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's broad 'fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for all functions' as accepted per-symbol match status because the bot report contradicts it for three functions.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not claim fn_8021E994 changed from the diff evidence; it appears in the PR body summary but not in the provided diff excerpt.
Do not promote grHr_804D6AE4, grGb_803E4048, or xC4/xC6/xC8/xCC meanings beyond the address/field evidence shown in the diff.
